observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineering Office to submit to the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as basis for its recognition in the books of accounts.
General Services Officer to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components; and secure a copy of the Road Map from the MPDO.
Inventory Committee to comply with the prescribed template on the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as illustrated in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008.
Municipal Accountant to prepare the ledger card of PPE accounts including road networks and henceforth, observe the proper accounting and reporting of road networks and the appropriate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the total road network system in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the management:
Submit justification for the non-withholding of the 10% Creditable Withholding Tax on the allowances and/or honorarium paid to judges, clerks of courts, and public attorneys.
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct the applicable withholding tax on allowances, honorarium, and similar payments subject to withholding tax as required under Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by RR No. 11-2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all the concerned officials to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, failing which shall be dealt with in consonance with Section 4 of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO and other concerned offices to review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan incorporating the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years as required by regulations and to address disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that management optimally utilize the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund by full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 for a disaster-resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that the management requires the inspection of the drainage construction and determine if it is long-lasting despite the shortfall of measurement and RCPC, and submit a report, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor, on the result of the inspection.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive exercise his responsibility in consonance with Section 444 (b)(1) and (b)(2) of RA No. 7160, also known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Management submit a justification for the delayed/ non-execution of the above-stated programmed projects.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) exercise general supervision and control over all programs, projects, services, and activities of the municipal government by ensuring that all approved and programmed projects are properly planned, executed/implemented and completed within the timeframe set to boost its economic condition and efficiency in local governance which benefits will redound to its constituents.
We finally recommended that the LCE ensure that all executive officials and employees such as but not limited to the Municipal Planning Development Office and Municipal Engineer’s office, of the municipality, faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided by law and cause the institution of administrative or judicial proceedings against any official or employee of the municipality who may have committed as an offense in the performance of his official duties.

(b) assign a responsible local staff who shall prepare the Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review (SAFSSR) and SAFSSR Report. The responsible local staff shall submit and present each SAFSSR report to the LCE and local Sanggunian. The FSSE findings shall be submitted and presented to the LCE and local Sanggunian to serve as basis in arriving at a decision on whether to continue LEE operations.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of PPE to determine the actual existence of all assets owned by the Municipality. They are required  to  submit  the  RPCPPE  on  or  before  January 31  of  each  year,  copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I;
Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), and to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer/Property Custodian to (i) maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger   Card   (PPELC)   for   each   category   of   plant,   property   and equipment, among others; maintain Property Cards (PC) for property, plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same (ii) reconcile their records for PPE accounts and determine the causes of the variance thereof and cause proper disposition; (ii) identify all PPE items without ARE and the employees accountable for the items to pave the way for the issuance of the ARE to all employees/officials concerned.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and effect the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the discrepancies noted.
Require the officer-in-charge or caretaker of biological assets to (i) maintain a Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC) to account any additions/reductions in the biological assets; (ii) prepare and submit a Quarterly Report of Biological Assets which shall aid the proper recording and reporting of biological assets to promote better accountability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management shall submit the lacking and other necessary documents relative to the procurement activities and progress payments for audit purposes and final inspection and evaluation of the project to avoid suspension in audit of the project.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to explain and justify in writing the noted deficiencies. Likewise, compel the contractor to cause the immediate rectification of the noted deficiencies for a given project, where applicable in close coordination with the COA audit team, taking into consideration the guidelines prescribed under prevailing procurement law and other applicable rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council to formulate projects to be funded by 20% Development Fund in consonance with the requirements prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 that is geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental goals that would uplift the quality of life of its intended beneficiaries.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management shall require the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to ensure that purchase orders and other pertinent documents are duly accomplished with the necessary information stating the date of acceptance or receipt by the supplier of its commitment to deliver the goods and services. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the same must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

Recommendations:
Direct all accountable officers to settle and liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and stop the practice of granting another cash advance unless previously granted amount was liquidated.
Send demand letters for immediate settlement to the accountable officers, particularly those who are not already employed with the agency.
For accountable officers who are currently connected with the agency and still have outstanding cash advances, the withholding of his/her salary may be enforced in case they refuse to settle their unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendation:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer with the approval and authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to set aside additional funds for the purchase of electrical transformer to start the operation of shredding machines as a useful tool in carrying out the waste reduction strategies of the Municipality in promoting a safer and sanitary environment.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to direct the Budget Office to explain in writing the excessive appropriation and to take appropriate measures in coordination with the Accounting Office in allocating the budget with appropriate timing as per amortization schedule in allocating the funds intended for paying off LGU debts in succeeding budget year. Likewise, ensure that available resources should be utilized to implement other important IPAs that are economically beneficial to its constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management that accounting and reporting guidelines should be strictly observed, particularly in the preparation of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMFIP). The LDRRMFIP should be properly accomplished with adequate and relevant information integrating the components of the funds, consisting of those held in the Current and Continuing Appropriation in the General Fund, Special Trust Funds and from other sources.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Planning and Development and Budget offices to install feasible mechanisms and appropriate measures in the planning and monitoring of Projects, Programs and Activities to be included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to maximize the benefits due to its beneficiaries.

Recommendations:
Require the Muncipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to conduct a comprehensive and periodic assessment of the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management operation against the LDRRMFIP before its submission to the MDRRMC for approval and cause changes whenever necessary, subject to availability of funds and
Exhaust all means to integrate and apply best practices of other agencies to be considered in the planning and budgeting of the MDRRMF.
Limit the utilization of the LDRRMF to projects and activities which are deemed relevant in  mitigating the risks of disasters.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
The Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Local College Board of Trustees may opt to:
designate a staff or hire the services of an external consultant who shall possess the necessary expertise and technical competence in formulating a detailed 5-Year Business Plan for the LEE, subject to the availability of funds. The designated staff or external consultant shall present the 5-Year Business plan to the LCE, stressing the importance of directing the LEE operations towards financial self-sufficiency;
(b)  assign a responsible local staff who shall prepare the Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review (SAFSSR) and SAFSSR Report. The responsible local staff shall submit and present each SAFSSR report to the LCE and local Sanggunian. Copies shall be furnished to the LEE manager who shall use the results of the reports in improving LEE operations; and
assign a designated staff or external consultant who shall be responsible for the preparation of the Financial Self-Sufficient Evaluation (FSSE) Report, together with an analysis on the financial self-sufficiency situation of the LEE as well as recommendation on whether or not the LEE shall continue to operate and undertake other related actions. The FSSE findings shall be submitted and presented to the LCE and local Sanggunian to serve as basis in arriving at a decision on whether to continue LEE operations.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management the following:
Require the Property Custodian to furnish the Municipal Accountant an updated inventory report  stating the list of donated items to facilitate the proper recording of PPE. Likewise, establish an Appraisal Committee to ensure that the fair value of the unrecorded PPE is determined accordingly.
Direct the concerned officials of the KNP to ensure proper documentation of all future donations from stakeholders by executing a deed of donation or an Acknowledgement Receipt for donated Property, Equipment, Supplies and Materials duly signed by the donor and KNP President, reflecting thereon the description and amount of asset being donated in compliance with the PPSAS requirements and other applicable rules and regulations. The same shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, together with the Property Acknowledgement Receipt and other necessary documentation, as basis for recording of the subject property, equipment, supplies and materials in the books of accounts. The donated items shall likewise be properly labled with the inventory tags bearing the official logo of the LGU.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the School’s Board of Trustees to require the assigned personnel-in-charge of the local college to cause the preparation and submission of a copy of the Assessment of All Enrolled Students to the Accounting Department with information on whether the student is covered by the Free Higher Education Program or not. The same shall be submitted on a timely basis or after a specific number of days from the completion of the enrolment period to ensure that accounts receivable is properly recognized, updated and reviewed regularly. Likewise, recommend to the School Board of Trustees or its policy-making body to evaluate the need to revisit the internal policies governing the appropriate accounting procedure and the timely reporting of these collectibles.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the local college administration to evaluate the collectability status of accounts receivables especially those that are long outstanding and to intensify its collection efforts by sending collection/demand letters to the concerned students.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management to:
Instruct the designated personnel of the school to discontinue the issuance of personalized Acknowledgement Receipts upon receipt of any other fees received from students and have it issued with authorized official receipt, preferably AF 51. Said collection shall be duly accounted and held as under Trust Liability and must be solely spent for the specific purpose for which it was created;
Direct the policy-making body to revisit their exisiting policies and procedures, or install appropriate controls whenever necessary for the imposition and collection of additional school fees; it must also ensure those policies and guidelines must be well-defined  and adequately communicated in clear and specific terms with written authority from its school Board of Trustees, subject to the prevailing laws and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following courses of action:
Direct the assigned personnel of the local college to stop the practice of demanding any additional fees unless prior approval by the Board of Trustees is secured;
Personnel in charge of the school shall consider the minimum requirements under R.A. No. 9184 as well as other applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations in processing disbursement of funds pertaining to any of its procurement undertakings.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
prioritize the remittance of the taxes withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to constantly monitor the monthly status of taxes withheld by the municipal government. Adhere strictly to the provisions in the BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and observe the practice of intact remittance of the monthly obligation to the BIR; and
ensure that transfers from the Other Funds concerning their tax remittances are properly recorded and reconciled with the General Fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
prioritize the remittance of the taxes withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and to constantly monitor the monthly status of taxes withheld by the municipal government. ensure that transfers from the Other Funds concerning their tax remittances are properly recorded and reconciled with the General Fund.

Recommendations:
Instruct the personnel-in-charge of the remittance to secure the NSA in a regular basis for the preparation of required ARA to forestall any delay in its remittances and ensure that any difference in salary due to step increment or other causes should be made before remittance is made.
Enjoin the personnel-in-charge to make reconciliation with the accumulated balance  for remittance as soon as possible. Also, all monies being withheld due the GSIS should be immediately remitted for the corporate’s timely usage of funds for the fulfilment of its plans, programs and activities.

Recommendations:
Instruct the GAD Focal Point person to hasten the gathering of data needed in the preparation of GAD AR and to submit the same to the audit team and other oversight agencies for evaluation purposes.

We recommended the following:

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records as soon as possible and to effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances.

To ensure that the same is clearly understandable to its interested users, disclosure of such relevant information must be duly supported with corroborating schedules that must be consistent with the reported balances shown in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Government Equity.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction:
Management still charged against the 20% Development Fund lump-sum appropriation for some projects implemented during the year.




Of the 14 projects held in lump-sum appropriation worth  Php14,013,148.44,only Php4,857,701.40 has been obligated and duly supported with an authority from the SB. Out of the 5 obligated projects,  copy of SB Resolution for the 3 projects is already provided. The management furhter clarified that SB authority for the remaining 2 projects is no longer needed since it is for the improvement of water system named Pantukan Water Works,  which is  managed and owned by the agency, hence it is considered specific and not a lump-sum appropriation. 

Disbursements charged to the fund for the said project is authorized under SB resolution per SB Res. No. 036, S-2018 and SB Res. No. 061, S-2018 copies of which was already furnished to the audit team.
observations.reason:
Due to nature of some projects, the agency still needs to have a lump-sum item appropriation. One of the projects that need to be in lump-sum appropriation is the rehabilitation of roads. The Municipal Engineer insisted that this project should be in lump-sum amount since roads that need to be rehabilitated are unpredictable. When a particular road has to be repaired and identified during the year, only then they will prepare Program of Works and other documents needed for the execution of the project. To comply with the requirement the agency seeks an authority from the SB as evidenced by a SB Resolution provided for this purpose prior to the the disbursement of the lump-sum items.

However, they are reminded that their present actuation is a violation of the existing regulation. To execute a project held in the lump-sum appropriation in the Annual Budget is still subject for  approval through SB Resolution.

observations.recommendation:
